42h  |

3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Continuation of Advanced Networks 1, covering advanced IP network concepts and important data-link layer protocols
Covers emerging network technologies such as SDN (Software Defined Networking) and NFV (Network Functions Virtualization)
Recommended Prerequisites
Knowledge acquired in Advanced Networks 1
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1: Advanced IP Protocols and Mobility
IPv6 and ICMPv6 protocols: structure, operation, comparison with IPv4
IPv4 to IPv6 transition: coexistence techniques (dual stack, tunneling, gateways)
IP Mobility: Mobile IP (MIP) protocol principle and operation
Introduction to Named Data Networking (NDN): content-based communication model
Chapter 2: Local Networks and Infrastructure Management
Switching and network segmentation: VLANs, VTP, DTP, STP (Spanning Tree Protocol)
Local addressing and media access: NAT, PAT, ARP, RARP, PPP, HDLC, CSMA/CD, LLDP
Network supervision and management: NTP, SNMP
Introduction to MPLS (Multi-Protocol Label Switching)
Chapter 3: Programmable Networks and Virtualization
Software Defined Networking (SDN): architecture, control/data plane separation, use cases
Network Function Virtualization (NFV): principles, technologies, complementarity with SDN
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Kurose, J. F., & Ross, K. W. (2021). Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach. Pearson Editions.
Bonaventure, O., Networking: Principles, Protocols and Practice, 3rd Edition, 2021.
Lannone, E., Telecommunication networks, O'Reilly Edition, 2017.
